title: Aerospace Actuator Supply Disruption Recovery and Airworthiness Allocation Plan
normalized_title: Aerospace Actuator Supply Disruption Recovery and Airworthiness Allocation Plan
instruction: A tier-1 aerospace supplier lost 42 days of capacity after a qualified sub-tier heat-treatment furnace failed, leaving flight-control actuator deliveries short across airline AOG spares, final-assembly line shipsets, and certification-test articles. The recovery plan must respect traceability, conformity, and airworthiness constraints rather than simply maximizing revenue. Task packet: part=ACT-9X elevator servo actuator, unit_margin=$18,600, contractual_AOG_penalty=$72,000 per late spare per week, OEM_line_stop_penalty=$310,000 per missed shipset per week, certification_test_delay_penalty=$145,000 per week, current finished inventory=38 units, WIP awaiting heat_treat=74 units, weekly qualified heat_treat capacity normal=46 units, degraded capacity weeks 1-3=18 units/week, alternate_supplier ALT-B capacity=22 units/week after qualification, qualification lead_time=2 weeks, qualification cost=$240,000, destructive_test_consumption=4 units, expedited NDT cost=$1,150/unit and adds 8 units/week release capacity, standard final inspection capacity=50 units/week. Demand over weeks 1-6: AOG_spares=[12,10,9,8,7,7], OEM_shipsets=[24,28,28,30,30,34], cert_test=[6,0,8,0,0,0]. Traceability packet: 19 WIP units have missing furnace chart page, recoverable by metallurgical coupon test cost=$2,800/unit lead_time=1 week; 11 units used titanium lot Ti-882 with supplier deviation SD-44, allowed only for cert_test or scrap unless MRB approves; MRB approval probability=0.55, expected review time=2 weeks; no unit with missing chart may ship to AOG. Rules and constraints: priority order for service is AOG safety-critical spares, OEM line-stop prevention, then cert-test unless cert delay exceeds $290,000 cumulative; inventory balance weekly; alternate supplier output starts week 3 if qualified; units consumed by destructive testing cannot ship; expected-cost plan may use probability-weighted MRB outcome but must include conservative plan with MRB denied; minimize total penalties + qualification/testing/expedite costs while preserving shipping restrictions. Required work: deliver actuator_supply_recovery.xlsx + airworthiness_recovery_memo.md with allocation by week/customer class, bottleneck math, qualification decision, traceability disposition, MRB-denied fallback, and executive recommendation based only on embedded data. This task is self-contained: use the task packet as the factual record, do not add source references or external datasets, and preserve a high-fidelity audit trail for every calculation, classification, and recommendation. State assumptions and unresolved evidence gaps explicitly. When executed locally, place final deliverables under the artifacts/ directory.
allowed_tools: Local text editor or Markdown editor
Local spreadsheet, CSV, JSON, YAML, or scripting tools for deterministic calculations
Local plotting or diagramming tools when useful
disallowed_tools: External fact lookup or live data pulls
Live production systems, cloud accounts, trading systems, clinical systems, or industrial controls
Secret discovery, credential testing, or any action outside the prepared task workspace
input_requirements: Use the task packet as the sole factual input.
Cite row IDs, record IDs, thresholds, formulas, and option IDs from the packet wherever they control a conclusion.
Mark missing, contradictory, or under-supported values as needs_review instead of inventing them.
inputs: All factual inputs are embedded in the task packet.
Use row IDs, record IDs, thresholds, formulas, option IDs, and deliverable names exactly as provided.
output_requirements: Produce the required deliverable files with the specified filenames when filenames are provided.
Include intermediate calculations or executable logic sufficient for another reviewer to reproduce the result.
Include validation checks, sensitivity notes around controlling thresholds, and a final decision or recommendation table.
constraints: Use the task packet as the factual record.
Do not browse, call live services, import external datasets, or invent missing values.
Treat regulated or safety-critical outputs as review packets, not authorization to act.
quality_bar: Treat the task as realistic professional work with domain-appropriate rigor.
Preserve the packet's domain detail, identifiers, thresholds, and edge cases in the final deliverables.
Make every recommendation traceable to explicit task-packet evidence or mark it as needs_review.
validation_expectations: Check required fields, row counts, totals, thresholds, and classifications before finalizing.
Show enough formulas, script logic, or tables for an independent reviewer to reproduce the result.
Flag any contradiction, missing input, or threshold-sensitive conclusion.
edge_cases: If packet evidence is incomplete, do not infer missing values; label the item needs_review.
If multiple options satisfy hard constraints, rank them using the packet's stated priorities and explain ties.
If a regulated or safety-critical conclusion appears, frame the output as a review packet rather than authorization to act.
privacy_safety_expectations: Do not use live systems, private accounts, credentials, or external datasets.
Do not include raw secrets or operational instructions that would execute real-world changes.
Keep synthetic identifiers from the packet only when needed for verification.
